title: rhus aromatica subsp. pilosissima
intro: Rhus aromatica subsp. pilosissima, also known as the smooth sumac, is a plant species native to North America, characterized by its small white flowers and red berries. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rhus aromatica subsp. pilosissima include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used externally to treat skin irritations and internally to support overall health.
